mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending capacity.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2017 payroll level 146,615k indicates sustained hiring momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with PAYEMS growth > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, the Fed enters a tightening regime that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as discount rates rise and growth expectations moderate.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2017 FEDFUNDS at 1.15% and CPIAUCSL at 244.048 indicate early tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,200 thousand units per month signal robust residential construction demand and forward economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through cyclical sector strength and leading indicator confirmation.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2017 HOUST at 1,215k indicates strong housing demand
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of months with HOUST > 1,200k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, signaling supply-side weakness and labor market softening that reduces corporate profitability expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2017 INDPRO at 105.16 and July UNRATE at 4.3% indicate stable supply conditions
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 1% while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,100 thousand units, indicating synchronized demand destruction across consumer and residential sectors that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2017 RSAFS at 478,873M and June HOUST at 1,215k show robust demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains below 1.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) stays below 2.5% year-over-year, indicating an accommodative monetary regime that sustains low discount rates and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through valuation support and reduced refinancing risk.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2017 FEDFUNDS at 1.15% and CPIAUCSL at 244.048 confirm accommodative conditions
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and corporate hiring expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through immediate sentiment shock.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2017 UNRATE at 4.3% represents stable labor market
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while real GDP (GDPC1) expands above 2.5% annualized, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and produce positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 1.0% AND GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:June 2017 INDPRO at 105.16 and Q1 2017 GDPC1 at 17,010.7B indicate solid supply conditions
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 63% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
